topic: React Server Components vs Astro Islands

sources.title: Patterns.dev: React Server Components Deep Dive
Web.dev: Rendering on the Web Performance Analysis

result.summary: React Server Components offer seamless full-stack React integration with streaming SSR and automatic code splitting, while Astro Islands provide framework-agnostic partial hydration with zero-JS-by-default, making RSC better for complex apps and Astro better for content-heavy sites.
key_findings: RSC reduces client bundle size by 30-50% for data-heavy components but still ships the React runtime (~40KB), while Astro Islands can achieve zero JS on static pages with selective hydration
Astro Islands support mixing React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid components on the same page, offering migration flexibility that RSC's React-only model cannot match
RSC's streaming SSR with Suspense boundaries provides superior perceived performance for dynamic apps, delivering 40% faster Time to Interactive vs traditional SSR in Next.js benchmarks
